title: OneSignal Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**OneSignal** is a customer-messaging platform delivering push notifications, email, SMS, and in-app messages through a single REST API. This connector — `OneSignalConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "onesignal"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Notifications | `/notifications` | Send, list, get, cancel push / email / SMS |
| Notification history | `/notifications/{id}/history` | Per-event CSV export (sent / clicked) |
| Apps | `/apps` | List, get, create, update apps (account-scope) |
| Players (devices) | `/players` | Create, list, get, update device registrations |
| Segments | `/apps/{id}/segments` | List / create / delete audience filters |

The connector normalises notifications + apps + players into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::OneSignalHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Collect your OneSignal credentials

1. Sign in to https://dashboard.onesignal.com.
2. Pick (or create) the OneSignal app you want to connect.
3. **Settings → Keys & IDs** — copy these two values:
   - **OneSignal App ID** — UUID.
   - **REST API Key** — long opaque string. Used to send notifications, manage players + segments.
4. (Optional) **Account icon (top-right) → Account → Keys & IDs** — copy the **User Auth Key**. Required only if you want to call `list_apps`, `create_app`, or `update_app`.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → OneSignal**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `app_id` (text, required) | OneSignal App ID from step 3 |
| `rest_api_key` (secret, required) | REST API Key from step 3 |
| `user_auth_key` (secret, optional) | User Auth Key from step 4 — only if you need /apps endpoints |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://onesignal.com/api/v1` (rare) |
| `timeout_s` (number, optional) | Per-request httpx timeout, default 30 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `app_id` + `rest_api_key` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `OneSignalHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /apps/{app_id}` using the REST API key. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
OneSignal REST API uses **server-to-server API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### The "Basic" prefix gotcha

OneSignal documents the auth header as:

```
Authorization: Basic <REST_API_KEY>
```

The header literally starts with the word `Basic` followed by a space and **the raw REST API key**. **The key is NOT base64-encoded** despite the prefix — this is a OneSignal-specific quirk inherited from legacy HTTP semantics. Standard HTTP Basic auth would require `base64(user:password)`; OneSignal does NOT. Any client that base64-encodes the value will get a 401 with `Invalid authentication`.

The connector enforces this in `client/http_client.py::_auth_headers`:

```python
@staticmethod
def _auth_headers(key: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
    return {
        "Authorization": f"Basic {key}",   # raw key — NO base64
        "Accept": "application/json",
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }
```

### Two keys, one header shape

| Surface | Auth | Key |
|---|---|---|
| `/notifications/*`, `/players/*`, `/apps/{id}/segments/*` | `Authorization: Basic <rest_api_key>` | App-scope **REST API Key** |
| `/apps`, `/apps/{id}` (PUT/POST) | `Authorization: Basic <user_auth_key>` | Account-wide **User Auth Key** |

The connector picks the right key per endpoint via the `_require_rest_key()` / `_require_user_key()` helpers.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `app_id` + `rest_api_key`. Initialises `OneSignalHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any OneSignal endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured `rest_api_key` (api_key flow has no exchange). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /apps/{app_id}` — minimal probe with the REST API key. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `app_id` | text | yes | `self.app_id` | Default OneSignal app UUID for shorthand calls |
| `rest_api_key` | secret | yes | `self.rest_api_key` | Authorization header value for app-scoped endpoints |
| `user_auth_key` | secret | no | `self.user_auth_key` | Authorization header value for `/apps` endpoints |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://onesignal.com/api/v1`) | Override for sandbox or proxy |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `self.timeout_s` (default 30) | Per-request httpx timeout |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["app_id", "rest_api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
ONESIGNAL_BASE = "https://onesignal.com/api/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `OneSignalConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::OneSignalHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `app_id` + `rest_api_key`; persist config. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo whose access_token is `rest_api_key`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /apps/{app_id}` with the REST API key. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Fetches the app record + recent notifications, normalises to `NormalizedDocument`, calls `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Notifications
children.content:
### `async list_notifications(app_id=None, limit=50, offset=0, kind=None) -> Dict`
`GET /notifications?app_id={app_id}&limit={N}&offset={O}`.

### `async get_notification(notification_id, app_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /notifications/{id}?app_id={app_id}`.

### `async send_notification(contents, *, app_id=None, headings=None, included_segments=None, excluded_segments=None, include_player_ids=None, include_external_user_ids=None, data=None, url=None, big_picture=None, send_after=None) -> Dict`
`POST /notifications` — payload built by `helpers.utils.build_notification_payload`; only set keys are sent.

### `async cancel_notification(notification_id, app_id=None) -> Dict`
`DELETE /notifications/{id}?app_id={app_id}`.

### `async notification_history(notification_id, events="sent", app_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /notifications/{id}/history` — body `{events, app_id}`. Triggers a CSV export of `sent` or `clicked` events.

children.title: Apps
children.content:
### `async list_apps() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /apps` — requires `user_auth_key`.

### `async get_app(app_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /apps/{app_id}` — prefers `user_auth_key`, falls back to `rest_api_key` for the connector's own app (used by `health_check`).

### `async create_app(name, gcm_key=None, apns_env=None, apns_p12=None) -> Dict`
`POST /apps` — requires `user_auth_key`.

### `async update_app(app_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PUT /apps/{app_id}` — requires `user_auth_key`.

children.title: Devices (Players)
children.content:
### `async list_devices(app_id=None, limit=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /players?app_id={app_id}`.

### `async get_device(player_id, app_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /players/{id}?app_id={app_id}`.

### `async create_device(device_type, *, app_id=None, identifier=None, language=None, tags=None, external_user_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /players` — `device_type` is the OneSignal device-type enum (0 iOS, 1 Android, 5 Web push, etc.).

### `async update_device(player_id, fields, app_id=None) -> Dict`
`PUT /players/{id}` — `app_id` is injected into the body automatically.

children.title: Segments
children.content:
### `async list_segments(app_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /apps/{app_id}/segments`.

### `async create_segment(name, filters, app_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /apps/{app_id}/segments` — `filters` is the OneSignal filter list.

### `async delete_segment(segment_id, app_id=None) -> Dict`
`DELETE /apps/{app_id}/segments/{segment_id}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
OneSignalError                         # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── OneSignalAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── OneSignalBadRequestError            # 400 — empty audience, missing app_id
├── OneSignalNotFoundError              # 404
├── OneSignalConflictError              # 409
├── OneSignalRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
└── OneSignalServerError                # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
OneSignalNetworkError = OneSignalServerError
OneSignalNotFound     = OneSignalNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BASE_DELAY_S * 2 ** attempt + jitter` (0.5s, 1s, 2s + 0..0.3s jitter), capped at 16s, retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.HTTPError` (timeouts / transport) | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `OneSignalNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED) with message text
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `app_id` or `rest_api_key` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. The REST API key is shown in OneSignal Dashboard → Settings → Keys & IDs. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — REST API key for a different app than `app_id` | Verify the key was copied from the SAME app whose UUID you entered as `app_id`. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — usually one of: (a) key revoked, (b) key base64-encoded by accident, (c) wrong header format | OneSignal expects `Authorization: Basic <raw_key>`. The connector sets this correctly; if you proxy through another client, ensure it does NOT base64-encode the value. |
| `OneSignalAuthError: user_auth_key is required for /apps endpoints` | Calling `list_apps()` / `create_app()` / `update_app()` without `user_auth_key` set | Open OneSignal **Account → Keys & IDs**, copy the **User Auth Key**, paste it into the connector's `user_auth_key` install field. |
| `onesignal.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (OneSignal per-app rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Batch your sends or use segments instead of long `include_player_ids` arrays. |
| 400 with `included_segments cannot be empty` | `send_notification` called with no audience | Provide at least one of `included_segments`, `include_player_ids`, or `include_external_user_ids`. |
| 404 on `get_notification` | Wrong `app_id` in the query string OR notification expired (OneSignal prunes very old notifications) | Verify the notification was created under THIS app and is within OneSignal's retention window. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'OneSignalNetworkError'` | Older code expecting the legacy name | Already covered — `OneSignalNetworkError = OneSignalServerError` alias in `exceptions.py`. |
| Sync syncs the app but zero notifications | App has never sent a notification, OR rate-limit retries exhausted | Send a test notification via `send_notification`, wait ~30s, re-run sync. |
